goals: Allow callers to request one or more SVG output sizes via a `--size` flag.
Automatically optimize every produced SVG with SVGO.
Surface all size variants in the JSON output via an `svg_files` array.
Keep `svg_path` / `svg_inline` top-level fields populated when only one size is requested (backward compatibility).
Expose the `size` parameter in the agent skill.
Document the new flag in all relevant docs.

userStories.title: `--size` flag on the CLI
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `zikon "app icon" --size 512,24,18` so that I receive one PNG and three size-specific, SVGO-optimised SVG files in a single command.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `zikon.js` accepts `--size <px>[,<px>...]` (comma-separated integers); default is `1024`.
PNG generation always runs at 1024Ă—1024 regardless of the requested sizes.
`imagetracerjs` traces the PNG once; the resulting SVG is the source for all size variants.
For each requested size, the pipeline rewrites the SVG `width`, `height`, and `viewBox` attributes and runs SVGO, producing a separate file (e.g. `icon_512.svg`, `icon_24.svg`, `icon_18.svg`).
Output files are written to `--output-dir` (existing behaviour).
Exit codes remain unchanged: `0` success Â· `1` PNG error Â· `2` SVG error Â· `3` invalid args.

userStories.title: Updated JSON output with `svg_files` array
userStories.description: As a developer or agent, I want the JSON output to list all produced SVGs so that I can programmatically access every size variant.

acceptanceCriteria.text: JSON stdout contains an `svg_files` array; each element is `{ "size": <int>, "svg_path": "<abs path>", "svg_inline": "<svg â€¦/>" }`.
When `--size` is a single value (including the default `1024`), `svg_path` and `svg_inline` top-level fields are still present and equal the single entry in `svg_files` (backward compatibility).
When multiple sizes are requested, top-level `svg_path` / `svg_inline` are omitted or `null`.
JSON is the only content on stdout; all progress logs go to stderr.

userStories.title: SVGO as a Node dependency
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want `svgo` declared in `cli/package.json` so that it is installed automatically with `npm install`.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `svgo` appears in `dependencies` in `cli/package.json`.
`npm install` in `cli/` completes without errors.
SVGO is invoked programmatically (not via shell) inside `zikon.js`.

userStories.title: `size` parameter in the agent skill
userStories.description: As an AI agent, I want to pass a `size` parameter to `/zikon` so that I can request multi-size SVGs without leaving the agent context.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `zikon-skills/index.js` `run(params)` accepts an optional `size` string (e.g. `"512,24,18"`).
When `size` is provided, it is forwarded as `--size <value>` to `node cli/zikon.js`.
The returned object includes the `svg_files` array from the JSON output.
Skill invocation `{ prompt: "app icon", size: "512,24,18" }` produces correct multi-size output.

userStories.title: Documentation update
userStories.description: As a user or agent, I want the docs to mention the `--size` flag so that I can discover and use it without reading source code.

acceptanceCriteria.text: `README.md` CLI usage section shows `--size` flag with example (`--size 512,24,18`).
`AGENTS.md` CLI interface table updated to include `--size`.
`.agents/PROJECT_CONTEXT.md` lists US-001 through US-005 of iteration 000006 under Implemented Capabilities.
Visually verified in a browser or markdown renderer that the docs render correctly.

functionalRequirements.description: `zikon.js` parses `--size` as a comma-separated list of positive integers; invalid values (non-numeric, zero, negative) must cause exit code `3`.
PNG generation resolution stays fixed at 1024Ă—1024; `--size` only controls SVG output dimensions.
The base SVG is produced by a single `imagetracerjs` pass on the 1024Ă—1024 PNG.
For each requested size, a new SVG file is written with `width`, `height` set to `<size>px` and `viewBox="0 0 <size> <size>"`, then passed through SVGO before writing to disk.
Output SVG filenames follow the pattern `<slug>_<size>.svg` (e.g. `minimalist_rocket_icon_512.svg`).
`svg_files` array is always present in the JSON output; it contains one entry per requested size.
Top-level `svg_path` and `svg_inline` are populated only when exactly one size is requested (default behaviour preserved).
`svgo` must be invoked programmatically via its Node.js API, not via a child process shell call.
The skill `run(params)` function passes `size` to the CLI only when the parameter is non-empty/non-null.
